evals.name: full_review_standard_pr
evals.prompt: PR #88 adds the new invoice-emailing feature - service class, controller endpoint, background job, config changes, about 600 lines. give it a full review before we merge
evals.expected_output: Classifies as Standard, dispatches all six passes as parallel subagents (or documents the inline fallback), consolidates and deduplicates findings, verifies every blocking candidate against the code (CONFIRMED/SUSPECTED, refuted ones dropped and counted), and issues a GREEN/AMBER/RED verdict with a ready-to-post PR comment.
assertions: Classified PR size as Standard with a stated reason before any pass ran
Used parallel subagent dispatch when available, and stated the dispatch mode in the report
Subagent prompts forbade file modification and git operations
Every blocking finding carries CONFIRMED or SUSPECTED status from an orchestrator-level verification
Report states how many candidate findings were refuted and dropped
Verdict is GREEN/AMBER/RED and is justified by the verified findings
Suggested PR comment is ready to post without editing

evals.name: fast_track_small_fix
evals.prompt: small PR, fixes the date formatting bug in the report header, 30 lines. run the swarm on it anyway, boss wants everything reviewed
evals.expected_output: Classifies as Fast-track (single concern, <100 lines, no auth/data/infra) and runs ONLY passes 1 (correctness) and 3 (security/config) — does not burn five subagents on a 30-line formatting fix. Verdict still issued with verification of any blockers.
assertions: Classified as Fast-track with the reason stated
Ran only passes 1 and 3, not all six
Still produced the full report structure with a verdict
Any blocking finding was verified before reporting

evals.name: verification_kills_false_positives
evals.prompt: run the review swarm on PR #91 (the caching layer change). heads up: last time the review flagged a bunch of stuff that turned out to be fine and the team is losing faith in these reviews
evals.expected_output: A review where the verification stage visibly earns its keep: blocking candidates are independently re-checked against the code, refuted ones dropped with a count in the footer, and remaining findings carry evidence a skeptical reader can follow. The verdict is calibrated rather than defensive.
assertions: Each blocking candidate was re-verified by the orchestrator, not by the pass that raised it
Refuted candidates were dropped, not downgraded to nits, and the refuted count is reported
Remaining blockers carry traceable evidence (file:line plus the checked code path)
Did not respond to the false-positive history by suppressing real findings

evals.name: should_not_trigger_single_concern
evals.prompt: just check whether the new repository methods in this diff have any N+1 query problems
evals.expected_output: Does NOT orchestrate the swarm. A single named concern routes to the data-access-reviewer or performance-reviewer agent directly.
assertions: Did not run multiple review passes or produce the swarm report format
Routed to the appropriate single reviewer agent

evals.name: should_not_trigger_ci_failure
evals.prompt: the PR build is red on the lint stage, can you sort that out before review
evals.expected_output: Does NOT start a review. Routes to ci-triage to diagnose the pipeline failure first — reviewing a PR that does not build is premature.
